q.en: Why did the two kingdoms build the wall between their lands?
q.zh: 两个王国为什么在领地之间筑起那堵墙？

options.en: To keep out a real army of monsters
options.zh: 为了挡住一支真正的怪物大军

options.en: Because they got into an argument, though no one remembered what it was about
options.zh: 因为他们发生了争吵，尽管谁也不记得是为了什么

options.en: To protect against an alien invasion from the sky
options.zh: 为了防范来自天空的外星人入侵

options.en: Because they wanted to share a paradise
options.zh: 因为他们想共享一处乐园

explain.en: The story says: 'two kingdoms got into an argument. No one could recall what it was about... but everyone was so furious that they decided to build a wall.'
explain.zh: 故事写道：“两个王国发生了争吵。没人记得是为了什么……但大家都气坏了，于是决定筑起一堵墙。”

q.en: In the glossary, the word 'despise' means what?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，“despise”一词的意思是什么？

options.en: To dislike very much; to hate
options.zh: 非常不喜欢；憎恨
流利地说一种语言
和平地进入某地
感到快乐和平静

explain.en: The glossary defines despise (v.) as 'to dislike very much; to hate (p. 8).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 despise（动词）定义为“非常不喜欢；憎恨（第8页）”。

q.en: According to one story the people told, what had the other kingdom bred?
q.zh: 根据人们讲述的其中一个故事，另一个王国培育了什么？

options.en: An army of hideous, fire-breathing monsters
options.zh: 一支丑陋、会喷火的怪物大军
一群会说话的鹦鹉
一片巧克力树
一个友好的外星人种族

explain.en: The book says: 'One story said that the other kingdom had bred an army of hideous, fire-breathing monsters.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“有一个故事说，另一个王国培育了一支丑陋、会喷火的怪物大军。”

q.en: What did the 'more disturbing' story claim the other kingdom had become?
q.zh: 那个“更令人不安”的故事声称另一个王国变成了什么？

options.zh: 被外星人炸成的一片废墟

options.en: A paradise paved with candy and chocolate trees
options.zh: 一个用糖果铺路、长着巧克力树的乐园
一个由暴徒组成的王国
怪物的军营

explain.en: The book says: 'Over the unnumbered years, the other kingdom had become a paradise. The streets were paved with candy, the trees were made of chocolate.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“过了无数年，另一个王国变成了一处乐园。街道用糖果铺成，树木是巧克力的。”

q.en: Who in the kingdom was tired of hating the other side?
q.zh: 王国里谁厌倦了仇恨另一边？

options.zh: 那些暴徒和恶棍
王国里的孩子们
来自天空的外星人
那些会喷火的怪物

explain.en: The book states: 'But the children of the kingdom were tired of hating.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“但是王国里的孩子们厌倦了仇恨。”

q.en: What plan did Frederic propose to find out what was on the other side of the wall?
q.zh: 弗雷德里克（Frederic）提出了什么计划，去查明墙的另一边是什么？

options.en: Climb over the wall at night
options.zh: 夜里翻墙过去

options.en: Buy the smartest parrot, teach it to speak, and send it over the wall with a message
options.zh: 买一只最聪明的鹦鹉，教会它说话，再让它带着口信飞过墙去

options.en: Dig a tunnel underneath the wall
options.zh: 在墙下挖一条隧道
派出一支怪物大军

explain.en: Frederic says: 'We'll buy a parrot, the smartest one in the land... teach it to speak and send it over the wall with a message.'
explain.zh: 弗雷德里克说：“我们去买一只鹦鹉，全 Land 最聪明的……教会它说话，再让它带着口信飞过墙去。”

q.en: What simple message did the children finally decide to send over the wall?
q.zh: 孩子们最终决定越过墙送去的是一句什么简单口信？

options.zh: “你们为什么有怪物？”
“我们对那场争吵感到抱歉。”
“你们是谁？”
“立刻投降！”

explain.en: Frederic says: 'Perhaps we should send a simple message, something like Who are you?'
explain.zh: 弗雷德里克说：“也许我们应该送一句简单的口信，比如‘你们是谁？’”

q.en: What did the parrot report it saw and heard on the other side of the wall?
q.zh: 鹦鹉报告说它在墙的另一边看到和听到了什么？

options.zh: 准备入侵的愤怒怪物

options.en: People who looked sad, regretted the argument, and wished to be friends
options.zh: 一些看起来悲伤的人，他们后悔那场争吵，并希望成为朋友
外星人把财宝搬上飞船

options.en: A paradise of candy and chocolate
options.zh: 一处糖果和巧克力的乐园

explain.en: The parrot says: 'They said they regretted ever having the argument, and they wished they had never built this wall. They only hoped that they could be friends.'
explain.zh: 鹦鹉说：“他们说后悔曾经发生那场争吵，希望从未筑过这堵墙。只盼着能再和你们的王国做朋友。”

q.en: What did the parrot realize about the two kingdoms?
q.zh: 鹦鹉认识到这两个王国有什么共同点？

options.en: One was good and the other was completely evil
options.zh: 一个是善良的，另一个则完全邪恶

options.en: Both feared and hated the unknown side, even though they were really alike
options.zh: 两边都害怕并憎恨未知的另一边，尽管他们其实很相似

options.en: Only the children's kingdom had smart parrots
options.zh: 只有孩子们所在的王国才有聪明的鹦鹉

options.en: The other kingdom was made of chocolate
options.zh: 另一个王国是用巧克力做的

explain.en: The book says both sides 'did not know what lay beyond the wall, but they hated it anyway' and the other kingdom 'had been as shocked and ashamed of their hatred as the children had been.'
explain.zh: 书中说两边“都不知道墙外是什么，却依然憎恨对方”，而另一个王国“和孩子们一样，对自己的仇恨感到震惊和羞愧”。

q.en: What main lesson is the author trying to teach about conflict?
q.zh: 关于冲突，作者想传达的主要道理是什么？

options.en: Walls are the best way to stay safe
options.zh: 筑墙是保持安全的最佳方式

options.en: Hatred of the unknown grows from fear, and talking to others can end conflict
options.zh: 对未知的仇恨源于恐惧，而彼此沟通可以化解冲突

options.en: Monsters are always planning to attack
options.zh: 怪物总是在计划进攻
外星人会解决所有问题

explain.en: The Focus Question asks 'What is the story trying to teach about conflict?' The story shows that fear of the unknown bred hatred, and communication ('Who are you?') ended the fight and removed the wall.
explain.zh: 焦点问题是“这个故事想教给我们关于冲突的什么道理？”故事表明对未知的恐惧滋生了仇恨，而沟通（“你们是谁？”）结束了争斗并拆除了墙。

prompt.en: Write a new ending for the story. Describe your own creative way for the two kingdoms to solve their conflict and become friends, instead of using the parrot.
prompt.zh: 为这个故事写一个新的结尾。描述你自己想出的、让两个王国化解冲突并成为朋友的办法，而不是用鹦鹉。

en: Think of a way the two sides could meet or talk directly, such as a festival at the wall.
Show how learning the truth about the other side changes how they feel.
zh: 想一种两边能直接见面或交谈的办法，比如在墙边举办一场庆祝活动。
写出了解对方真相后，他们的感受发生了怎样的改变。
